summary: A Next.js e-commerce site for tuxedo/mocassin shoes with a cinematic 3D landing page, a public shop with no checkout payment, and a WhatsApp-based sales handoff. Includes an authenticated manager dashboard for order tracking, product management and analytics.

tech_stack.framework: Next.js 15 (App Router, Server Components by default, Client Components for interactivity)
tech_stack.validation: Zod (shared schemas used for both API route validation and react-hook-form client validation)
tech_stack.ui_admin_only: Material UI (MUI X Data Grid for tables, MUI X Charts for analytics) — kept isolated to /admin routes to avoid CSS conflicts with shadcn
tech_stack.animation: Framer Motion (scroll reveals, page transitions, cart drawer, hover states)
tech_stack.3d: Spline — shoe and abstract guide character hand-modeled from primitives (Bend/Boolean operations), animated via States + a shared scrollProgress Variable, embedded in React via @splinetool/react-spline
tech_stack.state_management: Zustand for cart state (persisted to localStorage)
tech_stack.auth: Auth.js (NextAuth v5) with JWT session strategy — admin/manager login only, customers remain guests
tech_stack.image_storage: Cloudinary or UploadThing (Mongo stores only URLs, never binaries)
tech_stack.maps_location_picker: react-leaflet (OpenStreetMap, no API key) or Google Maps JS API

design_notes.color_direction: Neutral, editorial palette — cream/off-white background, deep charcoal or black text, one accent color (e.g. burgundy or brass gold) reserved for CTAs to evoke tuxedo/formalwear luxury
design_notes.typography: Serif display font for headlines (evokes tailoring/heritage), clean sans-serif for body and UI
design_notes.motion_principle: Motion should feel deliberate and slow (luxury pacing), not bouncy — ease-out curves, scroll-linked reveals rather than autoplay loops
landing_page_structure: Hero: rotating 3D shoe, brand name, one-line motto
Scroll section 1: Values — animated guide character gestures as text reveals word-by-word or line-by-line
Scroll section 2: Journey — timeline-style reveal, shoe rotates to show craftsmanship details (stitching, sole)
Scroll section 3: Purpose — closing statement, CTA fades in to 'Explore the collection'

phases.name: Project setup
tasks: Init Next.js 15 app with TypeScript, App Router, Tailwind
Install and configure shadcn/ui (customer routes) and MUI (admin routes only, scoped via route group)
Set up MongoDB Atlas cluster + Mongoose connection singleton (lib/db.ts)
Configure Auth.js v5 with Credentials provider + JWT session strategy for manager login
Set up Cloudinary/UploadThing account and upload API route
Set up Zustand store for cart with localStorage persistence
Configure environment variables (.env): MONGODB_URI, AUTH_SECRET, CLOUDINARY_*, NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER

phases.name: Data models & validation
tasks: Define Zod schema + Mongoose model: Product (name, description, price, images[], sizes[], category, isArchived, viewCount, likeCount, createdAt)

Define Zod schema + Mongoose model: Order (customerName, phone, deliveryLocation {lat, lng, address}, items [{productId, name, price, qty}], totalPrice, status enum ['new','contacted','confirmed','shipped','cancelled'], createdAt)
Define Zod schema + Mongoose model: AnalyticsEvent (type enum ['product_view','wishlist_add','whatsapp_redirect'], productId, createdAt, meta)
Define Zod schema + Mongoose model: AdminUser (email, passwordHash, role enum ['owner','staff'])

steps: Block out sole: flattened Cuboid + Bend modifier (X-axis, slight angle) for toe curve
Duplicate/shrink for a stacked insole/welt layer
Block out upper: Sphere scaled into an elongated oval, Boolean subtract with a Cuboid to cut it into a shoe-shaped dome
Apply Bend modifier to upper to angle heel down / toe up, matching sole curve
Boolean union sole + upper into one continuous silhouette
Add collar/opening: thin bent Torus or Cuboid positioned at the foot-entry line; Boolean subtract for an open cavity if visible in shot
Fake stitching: thin cylinders bent along the sole/upper seam, arrayed with small gaps (cheaper alternative: bake stitch pattern into a normal/bump map instead of geometry)

Material: Physical type, roughness 0.25-0.35 for patent leather sheen (0.5-0.6 for matte suede variant), near-black with slight warm undertone, boost Fresnel/reflectivity at edges for a premium (non-plastic) look
Rotate camera continuously while building to catch asymmetry/boolean seam errors before finalizing; keep pieces as separate named layers until proportions are confirmed, then do final booleans
Single Sphere, slightly squashed for an organic (non-perfect) silhouette
Two small flattened ellipses/dark spheres for eyes, offset slightly proud of the surface on Z
States: idle loop (squash & stretch bob between a slightly-taller and slightly-wider state)
Separate state for scroll-linked lean/tilt reaction
Create States for shoe: 'Base' and 'Rotated' (360° Y rotation)
Create States for character: 'Idle' and 'Reacting/tilted'
Expose a numeric Variable (e.g. scrollProgress, 0-100) in Spline's Variables panel
Link shoe rotation state and character tilt state to scrollProgress instead of Spline's native scroll trigger, so both objects can be driven from one external source of truth
Keep a separate on-load Timeline keyframe animation (scale up + slight rotate) for the initial hero entrance, independent of the scroll-linked states
Use Spline's Optimize panel (reduce texture resolution/geometry) before export, target under ~5MB scene size
Export as a Spline scene (.splinecode) via 'Export > Code Export > React'
Test scene on a low-end mobile device or throttled CPU before integrating

phases.name: Public site — landing page
tasks: Install @splinetool/react-spline and @splinetool/runtime
Build ShoeHero client component embedding the Spline scene via <Spline scene='...scene.splinecode' onLoad={...} />

Use Framer Motion's useScroll() as the single source of truth for scroll progress; on each scroll tick call splineApp.setVariable('scrollProgress', value) to drive both the shoe rotation and character tilt in sync with the on-screen paragraph reveals
Build scroll-triggered narrative text sections (values/journey/purpose) using Framer Motion whileInView, reading from the same scroll progress value

Add capability check: skip/replace the Spline embed with a static hero image + CSS-only rotation on low-end devices or when prefers-reduced-motion is set (check navigator.hardwareConcurrency / basic WebGL support)

phases.name: Public site — shop & product pages
tasks: Build /shop grid page: server-rendered product list, filters (price range, size, category), skeleton loading states
Build /shop/[slug] product detail page: image gallery, size selector, price, related products, 'add to cart'
Log product_view AnalyticsEvent on detail page visit
Add wishlist heart icon (persisted per-session in Zustand, increments likeCount via API)

phases.name: Cart & WhatsApp checkout handoff
tasks: Build cart drawer (Zustand state, quantity edit, remove item)
Build checkout step 1: delivery location picker (map pin drop or browser geolocation) + optional name/phone field
On submit: create Order document in MongoDB with status 'new'
Generate wa.me deep link pre-filled with itemized message (products, quantities, prices, total, Google Maps link to pinned location)
Log whatsapp_redirect AnalyticsEvent, then redirect browser to the WhatsApp link
Clear cart after successful handoff

phases.name: Manager authentication
tasks: Build /admin/login page (Auth.js Credentials provider, JWT session)
Middleware to protect all /admin/* routes, redirect unauthenticated users to login
Seed script to create first owner AdminUser with hashed password (bcrypt)

phases.name: Manager dashboard
tasks: Build /admin dashboard home: metric cards (orders in process, most liked product, live product count) using MUI
Build /admin/orders: MUI Data Grid listing all orders, filterable by status, click row to view full order detail (items, location map, contact info), manual status update dropdown
Build /admin/analytics: MUI X Charts showing most-viewed and most-liked products ranked, view/like counts over time
Build /admin/products list: table of all products with edit/archive actions
Build /admin/products/new and /admin/products/[id]/edit: drag-and-drop image uploader (native HTML5 DnD + Cloudinary/UploadThing upload), Zod-validated form for name/description/price/sizes/category
Implement soft-delete (isArchived flag) instead of hard delete

phases.name: Polish & launch
tasks: SEO: metadata per page, OpenGraph images, JSON-LD Product schema on detail pages
Rate limiting on public API routes (order creation, analytics events) to prevent spam/abuse
Accessibility pass on customer-facing pages (motion-safe fallbacks, alt text, keyboard nav)
Cross-browser/device QA, especially 3D performance on lower-end mobile devices
Deploy to Vercel, connect custom domain, set production env vars

recommended_v1.5_features: Admin roles: owner vs staff with different dashboard permissions
Order status change notifications (email or WhatsApp Business API webhook back to manager)
Discount codes / promotional pricing field on products
PWA support so mobile customers can 'install' the shop
Customer-optional accounts for order history (still guest-checkout by default)
Reviews/ratings on products once repeat customers exist
Inventory/stock count per size, auto-hide out-of-stock sizes
open_decisions_for_user: Choose image host: Cloudinary (more built-in transforms) vs UploadThing (simpler Next.js integration)
Choose map library: react-leaflet (free, no key) vs Google Maps (more familiar UX, needs billing key)
Confirm WhatsApp Business number and message template wording for the pre-filled handoff message
